headlines.title: 4 Million Barrels of Saudi Crude Set to Transit Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Two very large crude carriers loaded with Saudi crude are heading for the Strait of Hormuz in the latest sign of recovering traffic via the vital oil chokepoint. The tankers are owned by Japanese companies, Reuters said in a report citing data from Kpler and LSEG. According to th

headlines.title: Gulf Oil Exporters Slash Prices as Buyers Gain the Upper Hand
headlines.summary: Gulf oil producers are in a race to offer discounts to entice buyers, with Saudi Arabia’s latest price cut for Asian importers the sharpest in decades but unlikely to boost sales. Saudi Arabia yesterday cut its official selling price for crude to Asian buyers by as much as $11 pe

headlines.title: India’s Fuel Consumption Slips Despite Record Crude Oil Imports
headlines.summary: Despite recovering energy flows out of the Middle East, India’s fuel consumption last month fell by 3.7% from May to a total of 19.24 million metric tons, Reuters has reported, citing data from the country’s oil ministry. Fuel consumption was also down on the year in June, by 3.1

headlines.title: Why Abu Dhabi Is Building the World's First Integrated Energy-AI Economy
headlines.summary: For decades, countries have been competing by setting up larger energy sectors, deeper ports, or stronger digital economies. That era is ending. headlines.title: Big Oil Heads for Record Profits as Trump Turns Up the Heat on Gas Prices
headlines.summary: The oil majors are set for windfall earnings for the second quarter, which saw crude oil prices jump to a four-year high due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Saudi Arabia Ships 34 Million Barrels Through Hormuz Despite Thin Tanker Traffic
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia has shipped roughly 34 million barrels of crude through the Strait of Hormuz since the June 17 ceasefire, according to Kpler cargo-tracking data, even though only about 27 commercial vessels per day have been transiting the waterway with active AIS signals, according

headlines.title: UAE Oil Production Nears Record High After OPEC Exit
headlines.summary: The UAE is wasting little time capitalizing on its exit from OPEC, with crude production already climbing above 3.8 million barrels per day in June, its highest level in more than six years, as Abu Dhabi rapidly converts spare capacity into exports despite weaker oil prices. headlines.title: Russia’s Oil Windfall Vanishes as Urals Crashes to $42 a Barrel
headlines.summary: Russian crude oil prices have fallen back to where they were before the Middle East war, with its flagship Urals averaging just $41.66 a barrel during the first three days of July.
